BioCatch Push API Alert

BioCatch has proactively alerted the bank fraud system.

Mid-session signal transmitted: hesitation + segmentedTyping + excessiveCorrections
Score: 930 / 1000 ¬∑ Severity: HIGH

The bank has received this signal before any payment has been submitted. The fraud system can now intervene ‚Äî pause the transaction and trigger a safety check ‚Äî without waiting for the customer to hit Submit.

This is the Push API advantage. Unlike getScore which fires at payment submit, Push API gives the bank a head start. The session is still live. Sarah has not yet reached the confirm screen.

Bank Fraud System Decision

BioCatch Intelligent Friction
Triggered by Push API mid-session signal before payment submission
ü§ñ
bank_ai_agent_1.gif not found
Place the GIF in the same folder as the HTML file.
‚óè LIVE ‚Äî AI Safety Agent

Agentic AI Safety Check

The moment BioCatch sends the Push API signal, the bank can deploy an agentic AI agent directly inside the customer's banking app or web session ‚Äî pausing the session from proceeding until the check is complete.

What the agent does
Initiates a secure chat within the banking interface. Asks targeted questions to surface scam indicators: safe account transfers, staying on the line, urgency, coached responses.
What BioCatch watches
While the customer types their answers, BioCatch monitors behaviour ‚Äî response delays, typing hesitation, copy-pasted text ‚Äî all signals that the scammer is dictating the answers.
The outcome
If the responses or behaviour match scam patterns, the transaction is blocked automatically. No human agent needed. No waiting for payment submission.

RL

Scammer ¬∑ SPF Impersonator
Calm, authoritative, scripted. Uses fear and manufactured urgency as {{Sp}} primary tools. Never loses {{Sp}} composure. {{Ss}} has done this hundreds of times.
P
Priya
Senior Fraud Analyst ¬∑ 6 Years Experience
Your identity in Act 2. Uses BioCatch Analyst Station daily. Sharp and methodical, deeply familiar with what scam sessions look like in behavioural data.
About this experience: The choices you make affect the outcome. BioCatch signals appear in real time as you progress through the session. Every signal is based on documented product capabilities.

Act 1 ¬∑ Scene 2

The First Choice

The scammer's voice continues. The banking app is open. The victim's hands are slightly trembling.

Scammer
"I need you to open your banking application now. I will guide you through a procedure to secure your funds into a government-protected holding account temporarily. This will protect you."

As the victim, what do you do?

Branch B ¬∑ The Protected Path

The Victim Listened to Their Gut

Narrator
{{Vs}} lowers the phone. Hesitates for a moment, then dials a trusted contact.
Son
"Mum? Everything okay?"
Sarah
"Someone called me... from the police... they said my account is in trouble. They told me not to tell anyone-"
Son
"Mum, stop. Don't do anything. That's a scam. I'm coming over."
‚úÖ
{{Vs}} was protected, this time.
{{Vs}} had the instinct. Most victims do. But fear is a powerful override.

BioCatch doesn't rely on victims making the right call. It reads what happens inside the banking session, the hesitation, the trembling, the anomalies, even when the customer says nothing to their bank.

Branch C ¬∑ The Skeptic

The Right Question. The Wrong Outcome.

Sarah
"Can you send me something official? Your badge number, to my email?"
Scammer (without missing a beat)
"Of course, {{VICTIM_SALUTE}} {{VICTIM_SURNAME}}. I'll send it right now. Please check your inbox."
Narrator
An email arrives instantly, fake official letterhead, badge graphic, case number. Looks completely legitimate.
Sarah
"...Okay. I see it. I'm sorry for asking."
Scammer
"Not at all. Now please open your banking application."
‚ö†Ô∏è
The scammer expected this question.
{{Ss}} had the answer ready. Social engineering doesn't require gullibility, it requires only one moment of manufactured credibility.

Once trust is rebuilt, the victim proceeds. The session continues, and BioCatch is already watching.

Act 1 ¬∑ Scene 4

The Intervention

Score: 1000/1000. 10 signals confirmed. BioCatch has been alerting the bank since the Push API signal fired mid-session. The question is, what has the bank done with those signals?

PUSH API
BioCatch proactively pushes signals to the bank mid-session whenever a risk threshold is crossed, no payment needed. Bank can act at any point post-login. Fired once in this session, triggered by hesitative segmented typing on the amount field.
GET SCORE (getScore)
The bank calls BioCatch's API right before payment submission to get the current scam score. Acts as the last pre-payment checkpoint. Returned 1000 in this session.
BioCatch Scam Score 1000
üî¥ MAXIMUM RISK, 10 SIGNALS, PUSH API FIRED ONCE

How has this bank configured BioCatch?

Path A ¬∑ Real-Time Intervention

Push API + getScore

Narrator
The transfer confirmation screen suddenly changes. {{Vp}} screen reads:
"This transaction has been temporarily held for your protection. A DBS fraud specialist will contact you within 2 minutes. Please stay on the line with us."
Narrator, 30 seconds later
The customer's phone rings. A real bank number.
DBS Fraud Specialist
"Ms Tan, this is DBS. We've flagged an unusual transaction on your account. Are you currently on another call?"
Narrator
{{Vs}} breaks down. The scammer hangs up.
‚úÖ
Transfer blocked. Customer funds protected.
Score: 1000/1000 Push API: fired once getScore: BLOCK

Push API fired once, mid-session, after BioCatch detected the hesitative segmented typing on the amount field. The bank received the signal before {{Vs}} even reached the submit screen.

When {{Vs}} hit Submit, the bank called getScore, received 1000, recommendation: BLOCK. The transaction was stopped. Window from Push API alert to block: under 4 minutes.

Path B ¬∑ getScore Only, No Push API

getScore Worked. But Earlier Was Possible.

Narrator
When {{Vs}} hit Submit, the bank called getScore for the first time. The response came back: 1000. The bank's rule fired, the transaction was challenged, and the customer was shown a safety prompt. The system worked at the payment layer. But this was the last possible moment, not the earliest.
‚ö†Ô∏è
Transaction challenged by getScore. Intervention possible.
getScore: 1000 at submit Transaction: challenged

The getScore call returned a score of 1000. A safety check prompt was shown to {{Vo}}. The bank had the opportunity to intervene and block the payment.

This is a win, but it is also a late one. getScore only fires at the moment of payment submission.
Room for Earlier Intervention

With Push API configured, BioCatch would have sent a signal to the bank much earlier in the session, at the exact moment the hesitative segmented typing was detected on the amount field. That signal arrives before the customer even reaches the submit button.

WITH PUSH API
Signal sent mid-session, during amount entry. Bank aware before submission. More time to investigate, call the customer, or apply friction earlier in the journey.
GETCORE ONLY (CURRENT)
First signal arrives at payment submit. Intervention window is narrow. Victim is already at the final confirmation step under active scammer pressure.

Path C ¬∑ No BioCatch

No One Was Watching

Narrator
Transfer goes through. No alerts. No flags. Just a routine payment record in the system. The next screen: the customer calling the bank, voice shaking.
‚ùå
Transfer lost. Case opened.
No signals captured Full loss

Without behavioural intelligence, this session was indistinguishable from any other payment by a trusted, long-standing customer.

She had the right account. The right device. The right credentials. The right IP address.

The only thing that was wrong was how {{Vs}} behaved. And no one was watching for that.

Risk Signals ¬∑ 10 Detected
3 CRITICAL 6 HIGH 1 MED
isActiveCall CRITICAL 0:00
‚ñº
VOIP call active from the moment the banking app opened, sustained throughout the entire 18-minute session. First-ever instance in this customer's history. Victims in scam calls are kept on the line to receive real-time instructions and prevented from pausing to think or seek help.
screenBroadcast ¬∑ firstEver CRITICAL 0:00
‚ñº
Remote viewing app installed and connected for the first time on this device ‚Äî at the start of this session, before login. Scammer had full read-only view of the screen throughout. Every action was performed by the customer under verbal instruction. The victim retains physical control while under complete cognitive control of the scammer.
deadTimeAnomaly HIGH 1:10
‚ñº
112 seconds of near-zero touch activity on the login screen. Not a device issue ‚Äî a distraction signature. Hands were still, attention directed to the call. This extended stillness at login is highly anomalous against the established baseline.
loginAttemptAnomaly HIGH 1:52
‚ñº
Password entered incorrectly twice before succeeding. Zero failed logins in this customer's full account history. The errors reflect a deeply distracted user simultaneously processing verbal instructions from the scammer.
doodlingBehaviour HIGH 3:20
‚ñº
Circular non-purposive touch events across the dashboard. Not navigating ‚Äî waiting passively for the next verbal instruction. BioCatch identifies this passive-touch signature as doodling behaviour, distinct from purposive interaction.
highMinimiseMaximiseEvents HIGH 5:40
‚ñº
7 app-switching events detected. Customer switching to WhatsApp to read fabricated official documents sent by the scammer. First-ever occurrence of this pattern in session history. Each switch represents the scammer reinforcing cognitive control and maintaining the deception.
isLongSession ¬∑ guidedNavigation HIGH 8:00
‚ñº
Session: 18 min. Customer baseline for a full payment session: 3 minutes. 6√ó longer than baseline. Navigation path to Transfer section taken via a route never used before ‚Äî consistent with someone being walked through unfamiliar screens step by step.
clipboardEvent ¬∑ newPayeeAnomaly CRITICAL 10:30
‚ñº
First-ever clipboard paste event on this device. Account number pasted directly from WhatsApp ‚Äî not typed. Payee "Golden Trust Holdings" has never been transacted with. First-ever paste + unknown payee + active scammer context = high-confidence signal combination.
hesitationAnomaly ¬∑ segmentedTyping HIGH ¬∑ ‚ö° PUSH API 12:45
‚ñº
‚ö° This signal triggered the Push API. Average pause between keystrokes in the amount field: 14 seconds. Field cleared and re-entered 3 times. Each digit preceded by a pause ‚Äî the customer listening for the next number from the scammer before typing. The bank received this signal before payment submission and could have intervened.
dominantHandChangeAnomaly MEDIUM 14:30
‚ñº
Dominant hand shifted from established right-hand to left-hand pattern mid-session. Physical stress response under sustained psychological duress. Consistent with documented victim behaviour. Compounding signal ‚Äî not decisive alone, but adds weight to the overall picture.
dailyAccumulatedAmountAnomaly CRITICAL ¬∑ getScore 16:00
‚ñº
Transfer attempted: 45,000 (currency). Prior transaction maximum: 1,800. Ratio: 25√ó lifetime max. Bank called getScore at submission. Response: scamScore:1000 ¬∑ confidence:EXTREME ¬∑ recommendation:BLOCK. Transaction declined.

‚ö° PUSH API ¬∑ 12:45
Fired once. Triggered by hesitative segmented typing. Score at firing: 930. Sent to bank mid-session before payment.

data-i18n="pm.getscore_desc"Called at payment submission. Returned 1000/1000. Recommendation: BLOCK.

Score Accumulation ¬∑ 18-Minute Session
Score Progression

Key Insight
The score crossed the alert threshold (700) within the first 90 seconds of the session ‚Äî at the moment the active call was detected alongside screenBroadcast. This means BioCatch had actionable risk data before the customer even logged in.
The Push API fired at 930 ‚Äî 5 minutes and 18 seconds before the customer reached the payment confirmation screen. That is the window Push API creates.
Case Summary
Case type: Police / Authority Impersonation Scam
Fraud vector: Victim-led transfer under social engineering
Payee: Golden Trust Holdings (confirmed mule)
Amount attempted: 45,000 (25√ó customer max)
Outcome: Transaction blocked by BioCatch getScore
Push API window: 5 min 18 sec before submission
Traditional signals: All clean ‚Äî device, credentials, IP
Why Traditional Controls Missed It
‚úó Known device ‚Äî customer's own phone
‚úó Correct credentials ‚Äî no brute force
‚úó Familiar IP address and location
‚úó No malware ‚Äî genuine human interaction
‚úó No phishing link ‚Äî verbal manipulation only
BioCatch detected the anomaly in how the customer behaved ‚Äî not who they were.
Analyst Recommendation
Immediate
Block payee account Golden Trust Holdings across all customers. Alert risk team to FUSE cluster of 5 linked sessions.
Short Term
Enable Push API rule for active call + screenBroadcast combination at score ‚â• 700 to trigger intelligent friction earlier.
Strategic
Review Push API configuration across all scam typologies. This session showed a 5+ minute intervention window ‚Äî currently being missed by banks without Push API enabled.

Scene 6 ¬∑ Final Debrief

What BioCatch Saw

Sarah's full session, as BioCatch recorded it.

1000
MAXIMUM SCAM SCORE / 1000
10 SIGNALS ¬∑ EXTREME CONFIDENCE ¬∑ FIRST EVER FOR ALL HIGH-RISK INDICATORS
SignalObservationRisk
isActiveCall VOIP call active from app launch. First-ever call during a banking session. CRITICAL
deadTimeAnomaly 112 seconds of near-zero activity on login screen. Listening, not interacting. HIGH
loginAttemptAnomaly Password entered incorrectly twice. Extreme distraction at login, consistent with active call coaching. HIGH
doodlingBehaviour Circular, non-purposive touch events on dashboard. Sarah waiting passively under verbal instruction. HIGH
highMinimiseMaximiseEvents 7 app-switching events, switching to WhatsApp to view scammer-sent fake SPF documents. HIGH
isLongSession 18 min session, 6√ó her 3-min baseline. Guided navigation via anomalous route to Transfer. HIGH
clipboardEvent + newPayeeAnomaly First-ever clipboard paste. Account number supplied by scammer. New payee never seen before. CRITICAL
hesitationAnomaly + segmentedTyping 14s avg pause per digit. Amount field cleared 3√ó. Each digit preceded by a pause for scammer instruction. HIGH
dominantHandChangeAnomaly Hand dominance shifted mid-session. Stress-induced physical change under sustained duress. MEDIUM
screenBroadcast Screen cast to external display. First-ever broadcast. Scammer had full real-time visual access. CRITICAL
dailyAccumulatedAmountAnomaly SGD 45,000, 25√ó her lifetime max of SGD 1,800. 8-second long-press hesitation at final confirm. CRITICAL

Without real-time intervention, this session appeared identical to any other payment from a loyal, returning customer.

{{Vs}} had the right device, the right account, the right credentials, the right IP address.

The only thing that was wrong was how {{Vs}} behaved.
BioCatch is the layer that reads the how, not just the what.

Signal Glossary

isActiveCallA phone/VOIP call was active during the banking session, victim being coached in real time
deadTimeAnomalyExtended inactivity on a screen, user is listening to external instructions, not interacting
loginAttemptAnomalyMultiple incorrect login attempts indicating extreme distraction, consistent with active call
doodlingBehaviourCircular, non-purposive touch events, user is idle and waiting passively for next instruction
highMinimiseMaximiseEventsRepeated app switching mid-session, victim checking external content supplied by scammer
isLongSessionSession lasted significantly longer than user's historical average
clipboardEventFirst-ever clipboard paste on this device, account number supplied externally by scammer
segmentedTyping / hesitationAnomalyDigits entered in chunks with long pauses, each digit received verbally from the scammer
dominantHandChangeAnomalyDominant hand shifted mid-session, stress-induced physical change under duress
screenBroadcastDevice screen being cast to external display, scammer watching everything in real time
dailyAccumulatedAmountAnomalyTransfer amount far exceeds user's historical maximum, anomalous by 25√ó
Scam Score 1000/1000Maximum composite risk score. All high-risk signals present for first time simultaneously. Extreme confidence of social engineering scam.
Push APIBioCatch proactively pushes a signal to the bank mid-session when a threshold is crossed, no payment needed. In Sarah's session this fired once: after the hesitative amount entry (segmented typing + 14s pauses). Bank can act at any point post-login.
getScore (GET /v1/score)The bank calls BioCatch's scoring API immediately before payment submission to get the current risk score. The last pre-payment checkpoint. In Sarah's session: returned 1000/1000 ‚Üí BLOCK. Distinct from Push API, this is a pull, not a push.

This scenario is illustrative. All characters and events are fictional. BioCatch signal names reflect real documented product capabilities.

Before You Begin

A 10-minute interactive demo showing how BioCatch detects a police impersonation scam in real time, from inside the victim's banking session.

üé≠
Two Roles
Act 1 you are Sarah, the victim. Act 2 you are Priya, the analyst reviewing the session.
üì±
How It Works
Interact with the simulated phone on screen. A pulsing teal glow shows exactly what to tap next at each step.
üîç
What to Watch
The Scam Score starts at 600 at app launch. Watch it climb to 1000 as each behavioural signal fires.
Keep These in Mind
→Sarah passes every traditional fraud check: right device, right account, right credentials, right IP. BioCatch detects how she behaves, not who she is.
→The scammer views her screen but does not control her device. Every action is performed under verbal coaching.
→The Push API fires once mid-session before payment. getScore is called at the moment of payment submission. These are two distinct mechanisms.
→Watch for decision points throughout ‚Äî your choices lead to different outcomes. Try each path to see the full picture.
